reviews.review_description: feel guilty for brought on discount, it worth!

reviews.review_title: It's well made but really bland
reviews.review_description: My biggest issue here is everything is up to chance. You really don't feel like you have much impact on the outcome of anything. Maybe after further playthroughs it will reveal more nuance, but the gameplay feels and random as the pop culture references. I guess it's someone's bag but it didn't click with me at all.
I want a refund! Also, your view is drifting away all the time. Stay away from it. It hurts to your eyes

So far I am meeting new species and losing red shirt officers. Biggest peeve is the bridge, the crew members just repeat a very short. I would love a future version that adds in more interaction on the bridge but I enjoy where this is going so far.
Excellent mashup of Space Quest meets Oregon Trail!

reviews.review_title: Like Zork and FTL had a baby...
reviews.review_description: ... named Oregon Trail. It's a semi-linear roguelike a la FTL, with the dry humor people love from games like Zork or Monkey Island. Quite enjoyable, and it's never quite the same game twice. Definitely worth the $5.
Idea is good but is too easy, I completed the game the first time I played, you can't move from your chair, vr is unuseful
Please add a save and quit option so one can continue their play session at a later time.

Worthy of more than its current rating, Orion Trail is definitely worth your few dollars. Putting you in the captain's chair is a stroke of genius and makes for a quirky, funny, highly enjoyable experience unlike almost anything else you'll on VR. It's a very polished, well crafted game that I'm sure I'll visit again and again and will be showing friends and family.
If it wasn't for the VR aspect of this game, it would be pretty lame. The graphics are fun and I love seeing my crew vaporised on away missions.
beat the game without even knowing it was over extremely short very little to do enjoyed the concept of the game needs more interaction outside of read look and click

The idea of sitting in the captain's chair (that actually swivels!!) and looking around your ship and issuing your orders at the crew is a decent one for VR, but it never really expands into anything interesting and leaves all the gameplay up to chance. Imagine FTL with slightly funnier dialogue and no combat.
